title: A Darkening Year
prose: The Daily Prophet gets harder to read this year, not because of the writing but because of what it's reporting: disappearances, attacks, names of missing witches and wizards that keep multiplying week over week. Ollivander's shop in Diagon Alley stands shuttered and dark — the wandmaker himself gone, nobody quite saying where.

Security around Hogwarts tightens without anyone announcing it outright: more thorough checks at the gates, professors patrolling corridors that never used to need it. Even the Quidditch season feels muted this year, matches played under a kind of collective distraction nobody quite shakes off.

There's a specific, low-grade dread that settles into the whole school by winter — not panic, nothing so sudden, just a steady awareness that the world outside the castle walls is getting worse, and that the castle walls might not hold that fact out forever.

choices.label: Try to hold onto some hope regardless.
Start quietly bracing for it to get worse.
